articles.title: COMPLIANCE FOR EARLY STAGE STARTUPS
articles.summary: As a growing startup, building trust with your customers and partners is critical. And proving this trust via compliance frameworks like SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA, etc. can help your company raise capital, sell to larger customers, and rise above the competition. VANTA’S COMPLIANCE FOR EARLY STAGE STARTUPS pairs growing companies with one of Vanta’s trusted partners to help you implement and deploy Vanta, as well as aid your team in completing the tasks required to get audit-ready quickly.

articles.title: HOW MARKETS VALUE SOFTWARE COMPANIES IN 2023
articles.summary: Valuations are creeping back up based on more than just profitability. Things are picking up again and the guess is that things will pick up steam at the end of Q3 and go bonkers starting in Q4. Are we back? Maybe.

articles.title: VC FINDS ITS FOOTING AS HEADWINDS WEAKEN
articles.summary: Although venture capital investments continued to decline in Q2, the market is showing some signs of stabilizing.

articles.title: META LAUNCHES THREADS, ALREADY HAS MILLIONS OF USERS
articles.summary: Meta has launched its Twitter competitor Threads, and has already signed up tens of millions of users in the process, making this a good opportunity to get ahead of the curve on a new social network.

articles.title: WHY IT IS GOOD TO BE BAD AT ESTIMATION
articles.summary: Some good thoughts from one of the co-founders of Flurry on why it pays to be bad at estimating how long an outcome will take. This will likely (painfully) resonate with many founders on this newsletter list.

articles.title: SALES REPS?
articles.summary: Never hurts to have a refresher on thinking through early sales team makeup and distribution. This will change considerably in the next 12 months as new AI-powered sales tools permeate the existing process.

articles.title: SMARTEST FOUNDER IN THE ROOM
articles.summary: Go beyond competency and master the concepts behind today’s tech. Brilliant is the free and easy way to level up, with bite-size interactive lessons in math and logic, data science, CS, and more. Try it free for 30 days. [https://brilliant.org/TLDRFounders/]

articles.title: LET’S START RETHINKING WORK FOR THE MODERN WORKFORCE
articles.summary: The horse is out of the barn with respect to working remotely. The idea of going into the office 5 days a week just does not make sense. Rethinking remote work and how you can leverage it to your advantage is your only hope. A few thoughts from the team over at Hubspot that are, well, spot on.

articles.title: WHY YOUR STARTUP NEEDS A PODCAST
articles.summary: Every startup needs a podcast, and as Justin Jackson explains, it’s easier to start than you think.

articles.title: OPENAI MAKES GPT-4 GENERALLY AVAILABLE
articles.summary: Get out from under GPT-3.5 and start using GPT-4 via the OpenAI API which is generally available starting today.

articles.title: WHAT IS GROWTH?
articles.summary: A great piece on what growth means to David Norris. This one resonates a bit given the similarities in space-and-time juxtaposed with the craziness of a (yet again) disruptive technology. What is growth to you? It’s more than just a decrease in entropy.

articles.title: FAST
articles.summary: Some examples of people quickly accomplishing ambitious things together.

articles.title: MY TOP 10 MISTAKES GETTING TO $100M ARR
articles.summary: Jason Cohen, founder of WP Engine, discusses his top 10 mistakes getting to $100M ARR, including not raising enough money, prematurely trusting customer validation, and doing too much at once.
